turns.content: Why does it even work this way where I can just add two different numeric types together without writing separate logic for each one? It feels like magic that num-traits lets me implement Add, Sub, Zero and One on my newtype wrapper so generic algorithms can handle everything.

It's not really magic; it's the trait system. When you derive or implement traits like num_traits::Add from a crate like num-traits, you're telling Rust how to combine values of your newtype wrapper with other numbers via those generic numeric algorithms.

But I don't get it yet. If I implement Add on my custom type, why doesn't that mean I'm forced to write a separate branch for every possible pair of types? Isn't that what polymorphism usually looks like?

Exactly! That's the point. By implementing Add once on your newtype wrapper, you unlock generic numeric algorithms that work with any other type that also implements those same traits, so you don't need separate branches for every possible pair of types.
